data.body: FLIP CI (change 759) RED — Verified -1. LLM-Review +1. CI failure is NOT verify-identity — it is one INTEGRATION test: tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py::test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes (intended=250 persisted=144 lost=106 swallowed_write_raises=106). Root cause: that test spawns subprocess writers that write SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT events to a fresh store with NO signing configured; under require_authenticated=true the write-gate correctly REFUSES the unsignable writes and the drain swallows the raises -> the concurrency invariant (prune never drops writes) sees them as lost. It is a concurrency test, orthogonal to enforcement, that relied on the OLD default (require_authenticated=false). Fix (part of this flip): the worker _prune_race_worker.py must run enforcement-agnostic (set REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=0, like it already sets REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off). In production the review-bot signs (SSM key) so its writes are not refused. NOT a verify-identity/anchor problem — the anchor grandfathering is fine. Amending the flip change with the test fix + re-pushing.
